purpose: Fail-closed source, licence, attribution, and storage decisions for Country Climate Profile evidence.

policy.rule: Only an approved source with normalized_value_redistribution=true may contribute values to a public compiled country profile.
policy.raw_storage: Large or third-party raw files stay in versioned external storage. This repository stores manifests, checksums, reviewed normalized evidence, and reproducible retrieval instructions.
policy.licence_review: Approval is component-specific. Publisher-level terms never override a dataset, document, or upstream component restriction.

sources.title: Standard country or area codes for statistical use (M49)

licence.summary: The general UN website terms permit personal non-commercial copying but do not grant redistribution or derivative-database rights. No M49-specific open licence was found.
licence.attribution: United Nations Statistics Division, Standard country or area codes for statistical use (M49), retrieved {retrieval_date}.
restrictions: Do not imply that UN statistical groupings express a political judgment

approval.decision: Identity metadata may be linked, but copied M49 names, codes, and group flags cannot enter a public redistributed registry until reuse rights are confirmed.
approval.required_action: Obtain written permission or a source-specific open-data statement from UNSD; record the response and approved attribution.

licence.summary: UNFCCC states that official texts, data, and documents are public domain when unchanged and acknowledged, but Party-submitted NDC documents can carry their own copyright or reuse restrictions.
restrictions: Normalized target extraction requires explicit reuse approval

approval.decision: Registry metadata and links are usable. Public normalized target records remain blocked because Party-document terms vary and the general terms require unchanged content.
approval.required_action: Approve a fact-extraction policy with counsel or obtain UNFCCC clarification, then record per-document exceptions during NDC review.

sources.title: Biennial Transparency Reports and common tabular formats
sources.version: first BTR cycle; continuous reports snapshot retrieved 2026-07-15

licence.summary: Official UNFCCC texts, data, and documents may be copied unchanged with acknowledgement; report-specific and Party rights still require checking before normalized redistribution.
restrictions: Do not merge provided, mobilized, needed, and received finance
Do not silently reconcile official and harmonized values

approval.decision: Discovery and citation are approved; normalized policies, projections, tracking indicators, and finance facts are blocked pending reuse review.
approval.required_action: Resolve normalized factual extraction rights and document any Party-specific restrictions.

sources.title: National Inventory Documents and Common Reporting Tables

licence.summary: UNFCCC provides official submissions under its public-domain statement only when unchanged and acknowledged; source-document exceptions remain possible.
restrictions: Keep gas, sector, GWP, and LULUCF metadata
Do not republish altered source files

approval.decision: The official inventory plane requires normalized values, which are not authorized by the unchanged-document permission alone.
approval.required_action: Obtain a written interpretation covering normalized inventory facts or select an explicitly licensed official data interface.

sources.title: Technical expert review reports under the enhanced transparency framework

licence.summary: UNFCCC official texts and documents are stated to be in the public domain and may be copied unchanged with source acknowledgement.

approval.decision: Unchanged official review documents may be archived with attribution, but normalized review findings remain blocked.
approval.required_action: Approve extraction of factual findings separately from reproduction of the official document.

sources.title: Supplemental data of the Global Carbon Budget 2025

licence.summary: The ICOS collection record licenses all three GCB 2025 v1.0 spreadsheets under CC BY 4.0.
licence.attribution: Global Carbon Project (2025), Supplemental data of Global Carbon Budget 2025, version 1.0, https://doi.org/10.18160/GCP-2025. Changes and unit conversions by Earth Love United are identified.
restrictions: Preserve fossil and land-use series separately

approval.decision: Approved for normalized fossil and land-use CO2 evidence with versioned citation, attribution, and transformation disclosure.
approval.required_action: None; re-review on every new release.

sources.publisher: Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research / PRIMAP team
sources.title: The PRIMAP-hist national historical emissions time series (1750–2024)

licence.summary: Starting with v2.7, PRIMAP-hist is non-commercial and share-alike; commercial users need a separate licence, and TP-series commercial licensing is not offered.
licence.attribution: Gütschow et al., PRIMAP-hist v2.7 final, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17090760, retrieved {retrieval_date}. Preserve source citations and identify modifications.
restrictions: Commercial licence differs by CR and TP series

approval.decision: The project's future commercial context and share-alike compatibility have not been approved; no values may be published.
approval.required_action: Obtain a commercial-compatible licence or written legal approval for the intended public redistribution and downstream licence.

sources.title: EDGAR Community GHG Database: CH4, N2O, F-gases, and non-IEA components

licence.summary: EU-owned EDGAR_2025_GHG material is CC BY 4.0 unless otherwise noted. This approval expressly excludes the IEA-EDGAR CO2 component.
licence.attribution: European Commission, Joint Research Centre, EDGAR Community GHG Database, version EDGAR_2025_GHG (2025), {source_url}. Changes by Earth Love United are identified.

approval.decision: Approved only for explicitly EU-owned CH4, N2O, F-gas, and other CC BY components; ingestion must denylist IEA-EDGAR CO2 files and fields.
approval.required_action: Enforce the component denylist and re-check upstream notices at each release.

sources.publisher: International Energy Agency and European Commission, Joint Research Centre
sources.title: IEA-EDGAR CO2 component of EDGAR_2025_GHG

licence.summary: EDGAR states that its IEA-derived fossil CO2 component is licensed CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 and directs other uses to IEA compliance.
licence.attribution: IEA-EDGAR CO2 v4, a component of EDGAR_2025_GHG, based on IEA (2024) Greenhouse Gas Emissions from Energy as modified by JRC.

approval.decision: Normalization is a derivative and is incompatible with the no-derivatives term. This component must not enter public evidence.
approval.required_action: Use approved GCB fossil CO2 or obtain written IEA permission.

sources.title: Climate Action Tracker country ratings and current-policy assessments

licence.summary: CAT content may be reproduced and distributed with credit and copyright notice only for non-commercial purposes; commercial use requires contact.
licence.attribution: Climate Action Tracker, country assessment for {country}, version captured {retrieval_date}; © Climate Analytics and NewClimate Institute.
restrictions: Coverage is limited and continuously revised

approval.decision: CAT ratings and projections cannot be copied into the public profile until the site's commercial status and intended use are cleared.
approval.required_action: Obtain written permission or a licence compatible with public normalized redistribution.

sources.publisher: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division
sources.title: World Population Prospects 2024

licence.summary: WPP 2024 publications and data are distributed under CC BY 3.0 IGO with attribution.
licence.attribution: United Nations, Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division (2024), World Population Prospects 2024, retrieved {retrieval_date}. Changes by Earth Love United are identified.

approval.decision: Approved for year-matched population denominators under CC BY 3.0 IGO.
approval.required_action: Re-review each revision and preserve estimate/projection labels.

licence.summary: The WDI catalog entry identifies the dataset as public and CC BY 4.0. Individual indicator source metadata must still be retained because some series originate with third parties.
licence.attribution: World Bank, World Development Indicators, indicator {indicator_code}, release {version}, {source_url}, retrieved {retrieval_date}; original provider {provider} where listed.

approval.decision: Approved for WDI indicators whose catalog metadata confirms CC BY 4.0; third-party exceptions fail closed at indicator level.
approval.required_action: Validate the licence and provider metadata of every selected indicator.

sources.publisher: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development
sources.title: Climate Finance Provided and Mobilised by Developed Countries in 2013–2024

licence.summary: OECD generally permits data extraction, adaptation, and distribution with attribution, but warns that dataset-specific and third-party restrictions may apply. The report combines UNFCCC, DAC, export-credit, MDB, and mobilized-private-finance inputs.
licence.attribution: OECD (2026), Climate Finance Provided and Mobilised by Developed Countries in 2013–2024, https://doi.org/10.1787/ab5eb9ad-en, accessed {retrieval_date}.
restrictions: Do not infer provider-country values from aggregates
Keep provided and mobilized flows distinct

approval.decision: The aggregate report is citable, but its mixed upstream rights and aggregation are not yet sufficient for country-profile redistribution.
approval.required_action: Select exact OECD datasets/tables, verify their item-level licences and disclosure rules, and approve each finance type separately.
